text:
The Hunter Code (, Hantā no Fuchō) is a simple code used by Hunters to transmit secret messages to each other. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 383 

Description

When a sentence starts with a number, such as a time, each figure refers to a part of the corresponding sentence, whereas the rest of that sentence must be ignored. For example, if a Hunter prefaced their message with "13:44", the receiver would have to consider only the first part of the first sentence, the third part of the second one, the fourth part of the third sentence, and the fourth part of the fourth one. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

In the Black Whale, Mizaistom begins to suspect that the twins are planning an escape and, before Prince Fugetsu is escorted back to her cabin, he has an intermediary phone her room. He charges Keeney and Melody with putting a stop to any escape plans she might have, giving the command to the former. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 381 In reality, however, he spoke in code, requesting that the Hunters do everything they can to assist Kacho and Fugetsu. 

Practical Application Instances

 
Original Japanese text=
The message in code Mizaistom sent to Melody reads as follows:
 
「 カチョウ、フウゲツ両王子の 言動には不自然な点が多く警戒を要する
   何らかの方法で 逃走を 画策している可能性が極めて高い
   警護と並行し逃走防止にも 全力で 当たれ
   お前は責任者のキーニを サポートしろ！ 」
 
Since it was prefaced by the time "13:44", the parts to consider form "[カチョウ、フウゲツ両王子の][逃走を][全力で][サポートしろ！]" which loosely translates to "Provide full support to the two princes Kacho and Fugetsu to help them escape!". 
|-|
Viz translation=
Due to the message Mizaistom sent to Melody only been indicated as being coded two chapters later, most likely in the Viz translation the message was adapted without considering the Hunter Code and reads as follows:
 
" [1 st page, right text balloon:] 
There are oddities in the behavior of Princes Kacho and Fugestu. [sic] 
[left text balloon:] 
We must take precautions.
It's likely they're plotting to escape somehow .
[2 nd page text:] 
It is a key part of your duty to prevent that.
Continue assisting Keeney!"
 
If the code was transported to English in any way, only the following unlikely explanation could make sense.

Taking into account that the original time (13:44) was changed to "1:44 [p.m.]", the reader needs to read first the text balloon on the left then the one on the right (a reversal of the normal manga reading and probably a mistake) and ignore the rest of the message on the second page to make sense of it.

Since it was prefaced by "1:44", the parts to consider are something like: "We must escape somehow, Kacho and Fugetsu."

The Viz translation adapted this code later by changing the time and phrasing of the message to correctly convey its meaning. In Chapter 383, Hunters are to read the n th word in the appropriate sentence (in Japanese, it would be n th sentence clause) to get the true message. The timing of 11:55 was given, with the following message:
 
" [1 st page, right text balloon:] 
Assist Keeney, he's in charge!
[1 st page, left text balloon:] 
Princes Kacho and Fugetsu have been exhibiting odd behavior.
[2 nd page, right text balloon:] 
They're likely plotting to escape somehow.
[2 nd page, left text balloon:] 
Do your best to fully shut it down!"
